edges.description: Macrophage activation drives a T helper 1 (Th1) cell cytokine response.
Activated macrophages and Th1 cells drive amplification of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Sustained cytokine excess produces the systemic hyperinflammatory state.

nodes.description: Dysregulated innate immunity centers on activation of macrophages, the established central immunologic event that initiates the AOSD inflammatory cascade.

evidence.reference_title: Adult-Onset Still's Disease (AOSD): Advances in Understanding Pathophysiology, Genetics and Emerging Treatment Options.
evidence.snippet: The central role of macrophage activation, which results in T helper 1 (Th1) cell cytokine activation, is well established.

nodes.description: Macrophage- and Th1-driven inflammation amplifies pro-inflammatory cytokines, principally interleukin-1 (IL-1), IL-6, and IL-18, which are fundamental to disease onset and progression.

evidence.reference_title: Adult-Onset Still's Disease (AOSD): Advances in Understanding Pathophysiology, Genetics and Emerging Treatment Options.
evidence.snippet: Pro-inflammatory cytokines such as interleukin (IL)-1, IL-6 and IL-18 play a fundamental role in disease onset and progression.

nodes.description: The cytokine-driven hyperinflammatory state produces the characteristic systemic features (spiking fever, evanescent rash, arthritis) and, in proportion to its extent, variable multiorgan involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Adult-Onset Still's Disease (AOSD): Advances in Understanding Pathophysiology, Genetics and Emerging Treatment Options.
evidence.snippet: Adult-onset Still's disease (AOSD) is a multisystemic complex disorder clinically characterised by episodes of spiking fever, evanescent rash, polyarthritis or diffuse arthralgias; multiorgan involvement may develop according to the hyper-inflammatory extent.

nodes.description: Macrophage activation drives a T helper 1 (Th1) cytokine response, the adaptive arm that propagates the autoinflammatory cascade.

evidence.reference_title: Adult-Onset Still's Disease (AOSD): Advances in Understanding Pathophysiology, Genetics and Emerging Treatment Options.
evidence.snippet: The central role of macrophage activation, which results in T helper 1 (Th1) cell cytokine activation, is well established.
